report_name: TeamPCP Threat Actor Breaches TanStack in 'Mini Shai-Hulud' Supply Chain Campaign

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated threat cluster responsible for the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' supply chain campaign. The group focuses on compromising developer environments to steal secrets like API keys, private code, and cryptocurrency wallets by publishing malicious packages to open-source repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor compromised the TanStack open-source library and published malicious packages to npm and PyPI to be downloaded by developers.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely compromised a legitimate TanStack maintainer's account to publish the malicious package.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: The malicious packages used `postinstall` scripts in `package.json` to automatically execute commands upon installation, which downloaded a second-stage payload.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: The malware payload is designed to steal credentials from various sources on a developer's machine, including environment variables, configuration files, SSH keys, and browser data.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Stolen credentials and other sensitive information were exfiltrated from the compromised system to an attacker-controlled C2 server.

malware.name: Mini Shai-Hulud
malware.description: A credential-stealing payload delivered as a second stage after a malicious package is installed. It scans the compromised system for secrets and exfiltrates them to a C2 server.

campaigns.name: Mini Shai-Hulud
campaigns.description: A widespread software supply chain campaign attributed to TeamPCP, targeting developers by publishing malicious, typosquatted, or dependency-confused packages to npm and PyPI to distribute credential-stealing malware. A notable compromise in this campaign was the TanStack library.

identities.name: TanStack
identities.description: A popular open-source library and suite of tools for web application development that was compromised as part of the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' supply chain campaign.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: A public package repository for JavaScript that was targeted by the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' campaign with malicious packages.

identities.name: PyPI
identities.description: The Python Package Index, a public package repository for Python, that was targeted by the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' campaign with malicious packages.

infrastructure.name: Mini Shai-Hulud C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled server used to host the second-stage credential-stealing payload and receive exfiltrated data from compromised developer environments.

indicators.name: Suspicious Script Execution by Package Manager
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Script Execution by Package Manager
id: 2b1c8e9f-a0b1-4c2d-8e3f-9a0b1c2d3e4f
status: experimental
description: Detects a package manager (like npm or yarn) executing a postinstall script that spawns a shell. This is often used in supply chain attacks to download secondary payloads.
references:
  - https://cybermagazine.com/articles/this-weeks-top-five-stories-in-cyber
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.t1195.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\npm.cmd'
      - '\npm.exe'
      - '\yarn.cmd'
      - '\yarn.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\sh.exe'
      - '\bash.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate packages that need to compile native modules or run other benign setup scripts.
indicators.description: Detects package managers like npm spawning a shell process, a behavior consistent with the execution of malicious `postinstall` scripts described in the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' campaign.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Script Execution by Package Manager
